summary: First testing pass for ONCOST Catalog & Quotation app. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests passed (auth, products list/override/visibility, pricing rule recompute, quotation create/share/PDF/toggle/delete). Frontend: Playwright e2e covered login -> 92 product rows -> pricing rules page -> new quotation creation -> detail page -> public /q/<token> view with ONCOST branding & customer name -> PDF endpoint 200 application/pdf -> public /catalog with 92 articles rendered. All critical flows working.

minor.issue: ProductPatch filters out None values, so clients cannot explicitly clear override_price by sending null. Worked around by sending 0 (falsy) which then makes 'prod.get("override_price") or compute(...)' fall through to computed price. If a legitimate ₹0 override is ever needed, it would silently revert to the rule-computed price.
Public quotation response includes 'share_token' field in JSON (not a leak risk since the token is already in the URL, but ideally strip it from the response body for cleanliness).

issues: Product code SKUs are displayed as 'SG 501', 'SG 502' etc. The user spec mentioned 'no SG mentions visible' on ONCOST-branded surfaces. These are the actual product SKUs (carried over from source data), so it may be intentional, but worth confirming with the user whether the public-facing 'code' should be renamed (e.g., ONC-501) on the public catalog/quotation views.

action_items: (Optional) Allow clearing override_price via explicit null in PUT /api/products/{id}.
(Optional) Decide if SG-prefixed product codes should be re-coded to ONC-* for public-facing catalog/quotations (user mentioned 'no SG mentions').
critical_code_review_comments: compute_oncost_price uses 'prod.get("override_price") or compute(...)' which treats override_price=0 as 'no override'. Consider 'if override_price is not None' for correctness.
/api/auth/login sets cookie with secure=True, samesite='none' — only works on HTTPS; fine for the preview environment but worth noting for local dev.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (auth_testing.md guidance mentions 5-attempt lockout). Not blocking but should be added before production.
Quotations PDF endpoint regenerates PDF on every request — fine for current scale (92 SKUs) but could be cached if traffic grows.
server.py is ~640 lines — still manageable but approaches the 700-line guideline; split into routers/services soon (auth.py, products.py, quotations.py, pdf.py).
seed_data_creation: Backend autoseeded 92 products + admin user + default pricing rule on startup. Test created 2 quotations with customer_name prefix 'TEST_' and cleaned them up at end of test run. Pricing rule was temporarily changed to {500/25/200} and reset to defaults {1000/50/100}. Visibility & override_price changes were reverted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 13 backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_oncost_api.py and the full frontend e2e flow pass on the deployed preview URL. Backend cleanup is automatic at end of test run. Default pricing rule restored to {threshold:1000, below:50, above:100, rounding:1}. No TEST_ quotations should remain. Re-run via: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_oncost_api.py -v.
